page: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a developer-class virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, supported by 4.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 60 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is designed for light workloads. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance in the bottom 25% tier, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 30.95 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 18.54 GB/sec. However, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance in the middle 50% tier.

Given the shared CPU allocation and lower benchmark results for multi-threaded operations, this server offers a cost-efficient entry point for non-production environments. It is best suited for software development, testing, light single-threaded tasks, and basic utility hosting rather than resource-intensive database operations or high-throughput web serving.
description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a developer-class virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 60 GB local SSD. The instance features an x86_64 architecture and includes one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores in the bottom 10%, while single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores reach average levels. Due to its shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server is a cost-effective option for development environments, code compilation testing, and low-traffic utility hosting rather than high-performance database or production web serving workloads.
og_description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a KVM-based developer server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 60 GB SSD. It is designed for cost-effective development, testing, and light utility workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud DEV-2xCPU-4GB is a developer server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 60 GB SSD, optimized for light testing and development.
tagline: A budget-friendly developer instance featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for light testing and non-production workloads.

Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and a 60 GB local SSD
Delivers average single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address on a KVM hypervisor
Suited for software development, code testing, and low-traffic utility tasks
